description: R3a-2 summary-CORE + per-recursive-SCC fingerprint-TRACE goldens — ground truth for the al-sem → Rust differential harness. Each <fixture>.r3a2.golden.json is the ALLOWLISTED projection of the per-routine RoutineSummary CORE (dbEffects / uncertainties / parameterRoles / inRecursiveCycle / hasUnresolvedCalls), captured POST-computeSummaries (indexWorkspace → resolveModel → buildCombinedGraph → computeSummaries; the dump NEVER runs the dep hooks). Each <fixture>.r3a2-trace.golden.json (present only when the fixture has ≥1 recursive SCC) is the per-recursive-SCC fingerprint TRACE: the per-iteration stable fingerprint sequence + iteration count + the per-pass `changed` flag that the JACOBI fixed-point loop produced. The Rust port must reproduce the SAME trace → proves JACOBI parity (frozen prior-pass snapshot, not Gauss-Seidel). All ids in stable form (StableRoutineId / StableTableId / StableFieldId / stable operationId / callsiteId). See scripts/r3a2-projection.ts.
capturePoint: post-computeSummaries (indexWorkspace → resolveModel → buildCombinedGraph → computeSummaries; the final mutated routine.summary CORE; NO dep hooks)
note: R3a-2 is the summary CORE only. EXCLUDED (later gates): capabilityFactsDirect/Inherited + coverage (R3a-3, the cone), fieldEffects (lazy/detector), the dep hooks (R3a-4), full cross-app (R3a-5). computeSummaries DOES attach capabilityFacts*/coverage to each summary after the fixed point converges; the projection's strict allowlist drops them. The trace fingerprint is computed in STABLE-id space (al-sem's internal summaryFingerprint embeds internal RoutineIds/ObjectIds and is not id-portable); the internal→stable map is an order-preserving bijection, so the stable fingerprint changes iff the internal one does → the trajectory (iteration count + per-pass `changed`) is identical. Source-only: .app-bearing fixtures are EXCLUDED.

exclusions.reason: empty model (0 objects/0 routines) — fail-closed single-app provider; not source-only-analyzable
